body.title: Web UI: circuit breaker control panel
body.result: /api/circuit endpoint, rack UI toggles, Granny gate, CIRCUIT_OPEN channel posts; 6 tests
body.description:
Circuit breakers live inside each device's health panel section (not a separate tab). Each breaker is a toggle: CLOSED (running) / OPEN (paused). All breakers start CLOSED; on web startup, the server polls each device's /health endpoint to detect any pre-existing open state. Colored indicator: 🟢 CLOSED / 🔴 OPEN — text only for now, no fancy graphics.

CC.0 special breaker: activates stop_cc_minions (lab/claudecode/stop_cc_minions.py) to kill all cc-T-* sessions, AND sets a circuit_state.json flat file that Granny reads before each dispatch — if CC.0 is OPEN, Granny skips dispatch and posts GRANNY_THROTTLED|reason=circuit_open to channel. CC.0 CLOSED re-enables dispatch.

Device breakers: opening a device breaker posts CIRCUIT_OPEN|device=<id> to channel. BaseShim reads circuit state on each lifecycle iteration via a new check_circuit() method — if circuit is open, the device's run loop pauses. Web server /api/circuit/<device> endpoint reads/writes circuit_state.json.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (/api/circuit/<device> endpoint, health startup polling); unseen_university/shim.py (BaseShim.check_circuit(), health_surface() hook); devices/granny/daemon.py (check circuit_state before dispatch); lab/claudecode/stop_cc_minions.py (already exists); runtime root circuit_state.json
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; flat-file circuit state; log CIRCUIT_OPEN/CLOSE at INFO; OOP-first — check_circuit() in BaseShim, not per-device
**Scope boundary:** IN: CC.0 breaker + stop; per-device OPEN/CLOSE toggle; Granny checks CC.0 before dispatch; BaseShim.check_circuit() hook; startup health poll. OUT: automatic breaker trips (manual only), per-device custom circuit logic.
**Completion criteria:** Opening CC.0 kills cc-T-* sessions and Granny posts GRANNY_THROTTLED on next poll cycle. Opening a device breaker posts CIRCUIT_OPEN to channel. State persists in circuit_state.json and survives web restart. BaseShim.check_circuit() returns True (paused) when circuit open.
